lines.text: This is Personal Jarvis — a free, open-source AI assistant that lives on your own computer.
lines.tts: This is Personal Jarvis. A free, open-source AI assistant that lives on your own computer.

lines.text: You talk to it. It talks back. And then it actually gets things done.
This is the real desktop app — chats, agents, skills, a knowledge wiki.
And every result lands in one Outputs folder, ready to use.
Everything starts with your voice. Say your wake word, and just ask.
Jarvis answers in under a second — completely hands-free.
Ask for something big, and Jarvis sends real background agents — like Claude Code — to do the work.
Each mission runs isolated, and a critic reviews every result before you ever hear it.
It remembers, too. People, projects, promises — everything is written into a local knowledge wiki.
Plain markdown files on your disk — you can even open them in Obsidian.
The wake word and speech recognition can run fully local — your audio never has to leave the machine.
No account. No telemetry. Your files stay yours.
You bring your own keys — or a subscription you already pay for.
Gemini, Claude, OpenAI, OpenRouter — pick any brain, switch anytime, never get locked in.
Personal Jarvis. Free and open source.
It runs on Windows, macOS, and Linux — even on a five-euro server.
The link is right below. Come say hey.
